title: Add automatic content moderation for study groups

requirements.text: Define a predefined list of banned/sensitive keywords in the backend that includes profanity, hate speech, spam terms, and non-academic content indicators

quotes: predefined list of banned/sensitive keywords (profanity, hate speech, spam terms, non-academic content)

acceptanceCriteria: Backend contains a defined list of banned keywords covering profanity, hate speech, spam, and non-academic terms
The keyword list is case-insensitive when used for matching
The list is maintainable and can be extended in future updates

requirements.text: Implement automatic validation in the createStudyGroup backend method that scans the group description for any banned keywords and completely rejects the creation if sensitive words are detected

quotes: This check will happen automatically when groups are created

acceptanceCriteria: The createStudyGroup method validates the description against the banned keyword list before creating the group
If any banned keyword is found, the method returns an error result and no study group is created or stored
The validation is case-insensitive to catch variations of banned words
The error message clearly indicates that the group description contains inappropriate content

requirements.text: Update the frontend Study Groups page to display a clear, readable error message when study group creation fails due to content moderation

quotes: Real-time prevention so inappropriate groups never make it into the platform

acceptanceCriteria: When the backend rejects a study group due to banned keywords, the frontend displays a toast or alert with a user-friendly error message
The error message clearly states that the description contains inappropriate content
The Create Study Group dialog remains open so the user can edit the description and retry
The error handling uses the existing normalizeICError utility for consistent error display

constraints: Do not modify the study group data model structure; only add validation logic to createStudyGroup
Maintain the single-actor architecture in backend/main.mo
Use existing error handling patterns from the backend (e.g., #err for validation failures)
Preserve the existing Study Groups page UI structure and dialog flow
nonGoals: Admin tools for dynamically adding/removing keywords from the banned list
Retroactive scanning of existing study groups
Content moderation for other entities (posts, comments, messages)
Machine learning or AI-based content detection
Partial word matching or fuzzy matching for keywords
